entries.title: Early Progression
summary: A starter path that ties together the first tools, the named NPC houses around Starter Town, and the basic gathering loops that keep a new account moving.
It stays close to Starter Town and North Road Camp so the first upgrades, Talk-to detours, and combat drops come from the same small map slice the export already centers.

steps.title: Claim the basics
body: Start in Starter Town by making sure you have the universal utility tools in hand: a knife for shaping wood, a tinderbox for firemaking, and a hammer for later smithing.

While you do that, treat the named NPC houses as onboarding anchors. Use Talk-to on the people around the starter loop so the first quest-style leads and service roles are easy to spot before you leave town.

steps.title: Build the first gather tools
body: Use early woodcutting and crafting materials to make the first handles, then turn them into practical tools.
Do not separate combat too hard from this step. If rats, chickens, or goblins are sitting on the same route, fold their early drops into the same run so the export reads like one starter economy.

steps.title: Stabilize the loop
body: Keep the first run going by pairing gathering with bronze smelting, simple fishing, and enough cooked food to survive the outer pockets.
That makes it easier to branch into Tanner Rusk's leather-facing loop, the bronze gear route, and the first rune path without treating questing, merchants, and combat as unrelated systems.

entries.title: Hides Of The Frontier
summary: A beginner journey built around Tanner Rusk, the named NPC houses, and the frontier combat pockets that turn Starter Town into more than a pure skilling hub.

The export currently shows Tanner through leather stock rather than a raw-hide tanning recipe, so the guide focuses on how early drops, food, Talk-to NPCs, and leather crafting still form one onboarding loop.

steps.title: Read the town through NPCs
body: Open with the named houses in Starter Town, especially Tanner Rusk. The updated onboarding makes more sense if you use Talk-to as the first discovery tool instead of treating every service as a silent shop counter.
That habit is what turns the starter map into a quest path: the NPC names tell you which loops matter, and Tanner is the clearest signpost for the leather-and-frontier side of town.

steps.title: Work the frontier loop
body: Once you have a basic weapon and some cooked food, take short runs into the early combat pockets for goblins, boars, and wolves.
The important lesson is not raw efficiency. It is learning that early combat now feeds the same starter economy through meat, straightforward monster drops, and merchant-facing cleanup.

steps.title: Bring the haul back to Tanner
body: Bring the frontier haul back to Tanner Rusk and the crafting side of Starter Town. Even though the current export does not surface raw hide tanning as its own recipe chain, Tanner's leather stock makes the intended progression readable.
Use that moment to pivot into normal and wolf leather, then carry the loop forward into strapped handles, gear assembly, and the broader crafting economy.

entries.title: Bronze Gear
summary: The first real equipment path in the codex, built around bronze bars, basic forging, and the early strapped tool assemblies.
It is the cleanest entry point into the metal gear batch because the export keeps the entire bronze tier close together.

steps.title: Smelt the base metal
body: Mine copper and tin, then smelt them into bronze bars.
The bronze bar is the shared starting point for every bronze armor and weapon piece in this batch.

steps.title: Assemble the tools
body: Forge the bronze axe, sword, and pickaxe from their heads and blades, then finish the assembly recipes.
This is the first place where the export shows a clear bridge between smithing output and practical combat or gathering use.

steps.title: Finish the kit
body: Round out the bronze set with the shield and armor pieces once the resource flow is stable.
The export keeps these pieces simple on purpose, so the guide can focus on when bronze gear becomes the obvious early answer instead of the most efficient end state.

entries.title: Fishing To Food
summary: A food loop that starts with the simplest catches and ends with reliable cooked supplies for the early game.
It uses the exported Fishing and Cooking data together so the path reads as one loop instead of two separate skills.

steps.title: Pick the tool
body: Use a small net for the earliest catches or a fishing rod with bait when you want the rod-based loop.
Starter Town is the cleanest place to think about the fishing kit because the export keeps the supplier and teacher there.

steps.title: Cook the catch
body: Turn raw shrimp into cooked shrimp and raw trout into cooked trout as soon as you can.
The cooking side is the reason this journey matters: the value is not just in gathering food, but in turning it into something usable.

steps.title: Scale the supplies
body: As the loop opens up, fish toward salmon, tuna, and swordfish so the food shelf keeps pace with harder content.
This is the point where fishing stops being a side activity and becomes the most stable support skill in the starter export.

entries.title: First Runes
summary: The first runecrafting route in the export, centered on rune essence, the basic altars, and the pouches that expand each trip.
It leans on the starter map because the codex export keeps the mine and altar loop tightly localized.

steps.title: Mine essence
body: Start at the Rune Essence Mine in Starter Town and gather rune essence as the base input for every rune recipe in this batch.
This is the cleanest example of a loop where the world export, the skill export, and the item batch all point at the same activity.

steps.title: Unlock the basic altars
body: Use the ember, air, earth, and water paths to make the four basic runes first.
Those four are the backbone for the combo-rune recipes and the easiest way to understand the batch's runecrafting structure.

steps.title: Expand capacity
body: Pick up the pouches when you want longer essence runs and then branch into combination runes.
The export keeps the pouch ladder and the combo runes side by side, which makes this the natural place to push efficiency.
